# A url-encoded POST to a multipart route should not 400.
# ParseMultipartForm returns http.ErrNotMultipart for non-multipart bodies but
# still populates request.PostForm via its internal ParseForm call. The
# generated handler exempts ErrNotMultipart from the error capture, so text
# fields bind normally and file fields stay nil.

muxt generate --use-receiver-type=T
muxt check

exec go test -cover

-- template.gohtml --
{{define "POST /upload 201 Upload(multipart)" }}<p>{{.Result}}</p>{{end}}

-- go.mod --
module server

go 1.22

-- template.go --
package server

import (
	"embed"
	"html/template"
	"mime/multipart"
)

//go:embed *.gohtml
var templatesFS embed.FS

var templates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(templatesFS, "*"))

type UploadForm struct {
	Title  string                `name:"title"`
	Avatar *multipart.FileHeader `name:"avatar"`
}

type T struct {
	spy func(UploadForm) string
}

func (t T) Upload(form UploadForm) string {
	return t.spy(form)
}

-- template_test.go --
package server

import (
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"net/url"
	"strings"
	"testing"
)

func Test_url_encoded_to_multipart_route(t *testing.T) {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()

	var service T
	service.spy = func(form UploadForm) string {
		if form.Title != "hello" {
			t.Errorf("Title = %q, want %q", form.Title, "hello")
		}
		if form.Avatar != nil {
			t.Errorf("Avatar = %+v, want nil for url-encoded post", form.Avatar)
		}
		return form.Title
	}

	TemplateRoutes(mux, service)

	body := url.Values{"title": {"hello"}}.Encode()
	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/upload", strings.NewReader(body))
	req.Header.Set("content-type", "application/x-www-form-urlencoded")
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
	mux.ServeHTTP(rec, req)

	if got, want := rec.Result().StatusCode, http.StatusCreated; got != want {
		t.Errorf("status = %d, want %d (url-encoded POST should not be rejected as 400)", got, want)
	}
}
